sentences.grammarNote: **怎么样** (zěnmeyàng) = how's it going? / what's it like? — a very versatile greeting and evaluation question. More casual than 你好吗. **我是** (wǒ shì) = I am — **是** (shì) is the copula used for identity. Note: for names, 我叫 (wǒ jiào, 'I'm called') is also very natural.

**网站** (wǎngzhàn) = website — **网** (wǎng) = net/web, **站** (zhàn) = station/stop. The internet is **互联网** (hùliánwǎng) = interconnected net, or casually **网** (wǎng). **我的** (wǒ de) = my — possessive 的 (de) between pronoun and noun.

sentences.base_language: I like learning languages but it is very hard
sentences.target_language: 我喜欢学语言，但是很难
sentences.romanized: wǒ xǐhuān xué yǔyán, dànshì hěn nán
sentences.grammarNote: Chinese adjectives like **难** (nán) don't need a verb — 很难 (hěn nán) literally means 'very difficult' and acts as a complete predicate. **语言** (yǔyán) = language — **语** (yǔ) = language/speech, **言** (yán) = words/speech. **学** (xué) = to learn/study — 喜欢 + verb = to like doing something.

sentences.base_language: I am not very good at speaking spanish
sentences.target_language: 我说西班牙语说得不太好
sentences.romanized: wǒ shuō xībānyá yǔ shuō de bú tài hǎo
sentences.grammarNote: The **得** (de) complement structure: verb + 得 + result. '说得不太好' = 'speak not too well'. The verb is repeated before 得. **西班牙语** (xībānyáyǔ) = Spanish — **语** (yǔ) suffix means 'language': 中文/普通话 (Chinese), 英语 (English), 法语 (French).

sentences.base_language: I learned with other apps and they were great too
sentences.target_language: 我用其他应用程序学习，它们也很棒
sentences.romanized: wǒ yòng qítā yìngyòng chéngxù xuéxí, tāmen yě hěn bàng
sentences.grammarNote: **用** (yòng) = to use/by means of. **应用程序** (yìngyòng chéngxù) = application program (formal); casual: **APP** or **应用** (yìngyòng). **也** (yě) = also/too — always before the verb/adjective, never at the end of a sentence. **棒** (bàng) = great/awesome (colloquial).

sentences.base_language: But I made this app to do more "immersion"
sentences.target_language: 但是我做了这个应用来做更多的"沉浸式学习"
sentences.romanized: dànshì wǒ zuò le zhège yìngyòng lái zuò gèng duō de "chénjìn shì xuéxí"
sentences.grammarNote: **做了** = made (completed action — **了** marks completion). **来** between two verb phrases = 'in order to': 做了这个应用来做沉浸式学习 = 'made this app in order to do immersion learning.' **沉浸式** (chénjìn shì) = immersion-style — **沉浸** (chénjìn) = to immerse, **式** (shì) = style/type.

sentences.romanized: nǐ kěyǐ hé wǒ yīqǐ xué
sentences.grammarNote: **可以** (kěyǐ) = can / may (permission or possibility). **和我一起** (hé wǒ yīqǐ) = together with me — the standard structure for 'with me.' **一起** (yīqǐ) = together, always before the verb. The verb 学 (learn) is bare at the end — object is dropped when context is clear.

sentences.base_language: I tried to make this easy to use but I may have made mistakes
sentences.target_language: 我尽量让这个容易使用，但我可能犯了一些错误
sentences.romanized: wǒ jìnliàng ràng zhège róngyì shǐyòng, dàn wǒ kěnéng fàn le yīxiē cuòwù
sentences.grammarNote: **尽量** (jìnliàng) = to try one's best / as much as possible. **让** (ràng) = to let/make/allow — a causative: 让这个容易使用 = 'make this easy to use.' **犯错误** (fàn cuòwù) = to make mistakes — **犯** (fàn) = to commit/make (an error or offense). **一些** (yīxiē) = some/a few.

sentences.base_language: If you want to help me, that would be cool
sentences.target_language: 如果你想帮我，那太好了
sentences.romanized: rúguǒ nǐ xiǎng bāng wǒ, nà tài hǎo le
sentences.grammarNote: **如果...那...** (rúguǒ...nà...) = if...then... — a common conditional structure. **太...了** (tài...le) is a common intensifier: 太好了 = 'that's great!' The 了 at the end signals a changed state or adds exclamatory feeling. **那** (nà) here = 'then/in that case' (not 'that' the demonstrative).

sentences.base_language: If you like this website, please share it
sentences.target_language: 如果你喜欢这个网站，请分享它
sentences.romanized: rúguǒ nǐ xǐhuān zhège wǎngzhàn, qǐng fēnxiǎng tā
sentences.grammarNote: **分享** (fēnxiǎng) = to share — **分** (fēn) = to divide/share, **享** (xiǎng) = to enjoy. Together: 'divide and enjoy.' **请** (qǐng) before a verb = please (do something): a polite imperative. **它** (tā) = it (non-human referent). In casual speech, Chinese often drops 它 when context is clear.

sentences.base_language: If you know other languages, you can make lessons!
sentences.target_language: 如果你会其他语言，你可以创建课程！
sentences.romanized: rúguǒ nǐ huì qítā yǔyán, nǐ kěyǐ chuàngjiàn kèchéng!
sentences.grammarNote: **会** (huì) = can (a learned skill): 你会说中文吗？(Can you speak Chinese?). **会** vs **能** (physical ability) vs **可以** (permission). **创建** (chuàngjiàn) = to create/establish — **创** (chuàng) = to found/create, **建** (jiàn) = to build. **课程** (kèchéng) = course/curriculum.

sentences.base_language: If you can't help that is OK
sentences.target_language: 如果你帮不了也没关系
sentences.romanized: rúguǒ nǐ bāng bù liǎo yě méi guānxi
sentences.grammarNote: **帮不了** = not able to help — **V不了** (bù liǎo) is a potential complement meaning 'cannot accomplish': 做不了 (can't do it), 吃不了 (can't eat it all). **没关系** (méi guānxi) = it doesn't matter / no worries — lit. 'no relationship/connection.' A very common reassuring phrase.

sentences.base_language: I am happy you are learning with me either way
sentences.target_language: 不管怎样，我很高兴你和我一起学习
sentences.romanized: bùguǎn zěnyàng, wǒ hěn gāoxìng nǐ hé wǒ yīqǐ xuéxí
sentences.grammarNote: **不管怎样** (bùguǎn zěnyàng) = no matter what / either way — a fixed phrase. **不管** (bùguǎn) = regardless of. **高兴** (gāoxìng) = happy/pleased. **学习** (xuéxí) = to study/learn (slightly more formal than 学 alone) — **习** (xí) = to practice/review.
